section.section: Prerequisite ŌĆö clinical::api published prescription read + dispense port (inc-08a)
section.overview:
## What this slice builds

The cross-module seam that lets the (greenfield) `pharmacy` module read prescriptions, render the dispense worklist, and decrement clinical prescription state on dispense ŌĆö **without** importing any `clinical.domain` entity. Today `PrescriptionPort` lives in `clinical.application` (intra-module; the controller is its only consumer ŌĆö PrescriptionPort.java:11-21) and `clinical::api` publishes only consultation contracts (clinical/api/package-info.java:1-12). This prerequisite **publishes a new named-interface contract** in `clinical.api`, mirroring the `billing::api` pattern (BillingCommands / BillingQueries ŌĆö billing/api/package-info.java:1-16) and the inc-04 `SettlementDispatcher` event seam.

### The published surface (three concerns, one new edge)

Recommend **three** published interfaces in `clinical.api`, all uid-keyed, all returning published DTO records (no entity, no internal `id` ŌĆö ADR-0014 ┬¦1):

1. **`PrescriptionReadPort`** (read-by-uid + lot-trace read) ŌĆö `PrescriptionView getByUid(String prescriptionUid)`; `List<PrescriptionBatchView> listBatches(String prescriptionUid)`. Pharmacy reads the prescription to learn `medicineUid`, `qty`, `status`, encounter binding (consultation/nonConsultation/admission uid ŌåÆ patient type), `patientBillUid`, and the clinical-local `settled` projection.
2. **`PrescriptionWorklistPort`** ŌĆö `List<PrescriptionView> dispenseWorklist(WorklistFilter filter)`. **THE FILTER.** Reproduces the legacy bill-status worklist filter verbatim (see AC-RX-03/04). Returns only NOT-GIVEN prescriptions whose linked bill status passes the per-patient-type rule.
3. **`PrescriptionDispensePort`** ŌĆö `PrescriptionView markDispensed(String prescriptionUid, DispenseConfirmation cmd, TxAuditContext ctx)`. The decrement seam: pharmacy, after it has performed its OWN stock decrement / FEFO / stock-card write inside the caller's transaction, calls back to flip the clinical Prescription NOT-GIVEN ŌåÆ GIVEN and stamp `approved_*` + `issue_pharmacy_uid`. Propagation REQUIRED (caller's tx), NO `@Async`, NO `REQUIRES_NEW` ŌĆö atomic with the pharmacy decrement (mirrors BillingCommands.recordClinicalCharge ŌĆö BillingCommands.java:8-21).

### Direction of the seam ŌĆö no cycle

The clinical dispense terminal (legacy `issue_medicine`, PatientResource.java:3199-3293) did stock-decrement + FEFO + stock-card inline. In the rebuild that stock work belongs to `pharmacy`. So the **command edge inverts** relative to billing: pharmacy ORCHESTRATES the dispense (it owns the `pharmacyUid`-selected stock source ŌĆö Q2), and calls **down into `clinical::api`** to mutate the prescription. Add the Spring Modulith allowed edge **`pharmacy ŌåÆ clinical::api`** (read + dispense). Clinical must NOT depend on pharmacy ŌĆö there is **no `clinical ŌåÆ pharmacy` edge**, no reverse callback, no event from clinical to pharmacy. `ApplicationModules.verify()` stays green. This matches `registration ŌåÆ clinical::api` (clinical/api/package-info.java:6-8) and `clinical ŌåÆ billing::api`.

> **Modulith declaration.** No `package-info.java` change is needed in `clinical.api` (it already carries `@NamedInterface("api")` ŌĆö clinical/api/package-info.java:11). The three interfaces and their DTOs are added to `com.otapp.hmis.clinical.api`. The implementations stay **package-private in `clinical.application`** (e.g. `PrescriptionApiAdapter` delegating to the existing package-private `PrescriptionService`), exactly as `ConsultationLookupImpl` does (ConsultationLookup.java:16). The pharmacy module declares `@ApplicationModule(allowedDependencies = { "clinical :: api", "billing :: api", "shared", ... })`.

### DTOs that must NOT leak entities

New **published** records in `clinical.api` (do not reuse `clinical.application.dto.PrescriptionDto`, which is an application-internal type ŌĆö keep the api surface self-contained, mirroring `ConsultationDto` living in `clinical.api`):

- **`PrescriptionView`** ŌĆö uid; `status` (exact DB string "NOT-GIVEN"/"GIVEN"); `medicineUid`; `patientUid`; `patientBillUid`; `paymentType`; `membershipNo`; `insurancePlanUid`; encounter binding `consultationUid`/`nonConsultationUid`/`admissionUid` (exactly one non-null ŌĆö the patient-type discriminator the filter keys on); `qty`/`issued`/`balance` (BigDecimal); `issuePharmacyUid`; `settled` (clinical-local projection); `orderedAt`/`approvedAt`. Field-for-field a published mirror of the as-built `PrescriptionDto` (PrescriptionDto.java:18-81) minus nothing load-bearing; **no `id`**.
- **`PrescriptionBatchView`** ŌĆö uid, prescriptionUid, no, manufacturedDate, expiryDate, qty, createdAt (mirror of PrescriptionBatchDto.java:14-25). Read-only lot trace.
- **`WorklistFilter`** ŌĆö the filter input: optional `patientType` (OUTPATIENT/OUTSIDER/INPATIENT ŌĆö published enum, NOT the domain status), optional `patientUid`, optional `pharmacyUid` context. The filter is applied **server-side in clinical** against the bill-status rule; pharmacy never sees unsettled rows it is not entitled to.
- **`DispenseConfirmation`** ŌĆö `BigDecimal issued`, `String issuePharmacyUid` (required here ŌĆö the server-validated `pharmacyUid`, Q2), optional lot-trace lines to persist as `PrescriptionBatch` rows. Mirrors the as-built `IssueRequest` (IssueRequest.java:20-35) but `issuePharmacyUid` is **required** at this seam because pharmacy has already resolved/validated it.

### THE reconciliation flag (must go to BA) ŌĆö as-built worklist contradicts the ratified FILTER

**This is the load-bearing finding.** The as-built code in clinical **deliberately drops the settled/bill-status filter** for the pharmacy worklist:

- `PrescriptionPort.pharmacyWorklist()` javadoc: *"Returns all NOT-GIVEN prescriptions regardless of settled flag (the pharmacist validates payment physically before dispensing)."* ŌĆö PrescriptionPort.java:124-131.
- `PrescriptionRepository.findByStatusOrderByCreatedAtAsc` javadoc: *"The settled filter is intentionally omitted here: the pharmacy sees ALL pending orders ŌĆ” the pharmacist validates payment before dispensing physically."* ŌĆö PrescriptionRepository.java:132-143.
- `PrescriptionService.pharmacyWorklist()` returns `findByStatusOrderByCreatedAtAsc(NOT_GIVEN)` with no bill-status predicate ŌĆö PrescriptionService.java:362-366.

This **contradicts** ratified **Q1** (DECISIONS-RATIFIED.md:16) and the verified legacy behaviour: legacy enforces payment **only** in the worklist filter (it is a FILTER, not a terminal gate), and that filter is **not empty** ŌĆö OUTPATIENT/OUTSIDER require bill `PAID|COVERED`, INPATIENT additionally admits `VERIFIED` (PatientResource.java:4347, :4364, :4381, :4410; RECONCILIATION ┬¦C, ┬¦J/D13; discovery-raw topic "CASH pay gate for clinical prescriptions = WORKLIST FILTER"). The as-built "return everything NOT-GIVEN" worklist is therefore a **silent behaviour change** (an un-CR'd deviation) sitting in inc-05 code, and inc-08a must NOT freeze on top of it.

**Resolution for the new seam:** `PrescriptionWorklistPort.dispenseWorklist` reproduces the legacy filter **verbatim** (AC-RX-03/04). The inc-05 intra-module `pharmacyWorklist()` and its repository finder/javadoc must be reconciled ŌĆö **flag to BA + engagement-lead**: either (a) the inc-05 worklist comment is wrong and must be corrected to the legacy filter (most likely ŌĆö it asserts a process change with no legacy basis), or (b) there is a BA-confirmed approved change to show-all-then-validate-physically, which would need an explicit CR. Until BA rules, **the published seam is specified to the legacy filter** and the parity oracle asserts the filter, not show-all. This is the inc-06 "hard gate vs filter" lesson applied again (RECONCILIATION N13): do not collapse a non-empty FILTER into "show everything."

### What this seam explicitly does NOT do (honouring the hard rules)

- **No hard dispense gate** at `markDispensed` (Q1, DECISIONS-RATIFIED.md:16, :37): no `BillingQueries.getBillStatus` call, no bill-status re-check at the dispense seam. Pay enforcement is the worklist FILTER only. If an owner ever elects a hard gate it is a `SettlementPolicy.requireSettled()` local-flag form ŌĆö never wired into this port now.
- **No `pharmacyUid` affiliation check** (Q2, DECISIONS-RATIFIED.md:17): the `issuePharmacyUid`/`pharmacyUid` is a required, server-validated stock-source selector. This seam does not check userŌåÆpharmacy affiliation and introduces no `pharmacy_staff` table/claim.
- **No FEFO / NULLS-LAST / lock semantics** are expressed in this seam ŌĆö those are pharmacy-module concerns and are BLOCKED pending ADR-0017 + HDE (Q4/Q8). The clinical seam only flips NOT-GIVEN ŌåÆ GIVEN and persists the lot-trace rows pharmacy supplies.
- **No new prescription states.** Only NOT-GIVEN ŌåÆ GIVEN exists (PrescriptionStatus.java:23-26); the seam must not introduce ACCEPTED/HELD/VERIFIED/SOLD (D1 phantom).

acceptanceCriteria.decisionRef: ADR-0014 ┬¦1 (no id leak); RECONCILIATION ground-truth A1/A2

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: GIVEN OUTPATIENT or OUTSIDER prescriptions WHEN PrescriptionWorklistPort.dispenseWorklist is called THEN it returns ONLY status=NOT-GIVEN prescriptions whose linked PatientBill status is PAID or COVERED (NOT-GIVEN-but-UNPAID and NOT-GIVEN-but-VERIFIED are excluded for these patient types), ordered oldest-first (FIFO); it is a FILTER, not a terminal gate.

check.reason: The section is charter-compliant on all three audit axes: (1) it smuggles NO deviation into the frozen baseline ŌĆö AC-RX-06 explicitly forbids a hard dispense gate / BillingQueries.getBillStatus at markDispensed, AC-RX-09 excludes pharmacy_staff/affiliation (Q2), AC-RX-10 holds NOT-GIVEN/GIVEN only (D1 phantom rejected), and no "177", server-authoritative RO numbers, per-transition SoD, or OTC PK-suffix appears (correctly, as this is the clinical-seam prereq, not those slices); (2) every frozen:true criterion is backed by a verified legacy cite or a legitimate modern data-type/boundary change AND a ratified decision ŌĆö I confirmed the worklist filter (PatientResource.java:4347/4364/4381/4410), the dispense guard order (as-built Prescription.issue() lines 514-547 mirroring PatientResource.java:3199-3293), two-state PrescriptionStatus.java:23-26, the no-id PrescriptionDto/PrescriptionBatchDto mirror (ADR-0014), the billing::api/ConsultationLookup published-interface + package-private-Impl pattern, and the REQUIRED-propagation rule (BillingCommands.java:8-21); (3) the BLOCKED items are correctly frozen:false ŌĆö AC-RX-05 (worklist contradiction) and AC-RX-11 (FEFO/lock/NULLS-LAST, Q4+Q8). The AC-RX-05 finding is real and load-bearing: as-built PrescriptionPort.pharmacyWorklist() (124-131), PrescriptionRepository.findByStatusOrderByCreatedAtAsc javadoc (132-143), and PrescriptionService.pharmacyWorklist() (362-366) deliberately drop the bill-status filter, contradicting ratified Q1 ŌĆö verified verbatim. I confirmed SettlementPolicy.requireSettled() (SettlementPolicy.java:60-65) exists as the local-flag mechanism blockedItems[2] correctly names as the ONLY permitted future-gate form. I issue REVISE rather than UPHELD solely because AC-RX-06 mis-states the direction of a safety-critical clinical guard: it writes "issued<=balance" but the as-built (Prescription.java:524-526) and the legacy cite (PatientResource.java:3221) reject the UNDER-issue case (issued < balance ŌåÆ reject), i.e. the guard is "issued >= balance". The guards collapse to issued==qty in practice (balance==qty at creation, guard 4 forces issued==qty), so it is non-load-bearing ŌĆö but clinical/financial guard text must read exactly to the legacy cite, so it must be corrected before freeze.
corrections: AC-RX-06: correct the guard-direction text. As written it lists 'issued<=balance' as a guard; the as-built Prescription.issue() (Prescription.java:524-526) and the legacy cite (PatientResource.java:3221) reject the UNDER-issue case via `if (issuedQty.compareTo(this.balance) < 0) throw`, i.e. the guard is 'issued >= balance (reject under-issue)' not 'issued <= balance'. Restate as the verified legacy order: (1) status==NOT-GIVEN else 'not a pending prescription'; (2) issued>0 else 'Invalid issue value'; (3) issued >= balance else 'Invalid issue value' (under-issue rejected); (4) issued==qty else 'You can only issue the prescribed qty'. Note that (2)-(4) collapse to issued==qty because balance==qty at creation; keep the issued==qty cite PatientResource.java:3224 (the draft's :3230 is the legacy line for the same all-or-nothing rule ŌĆö align the cite to the one the as-built comment uses, PatientResource.java:3224).

AC-RX-06 (non-blocking nicety, fold into the same fix): the criterion phrasing 'issued<=balance' could be misread as permitting partial issue up to balance, which would be a silent behaviour drift from the all-or-nothing legacy rule. Make the all-or-nothing intent explicit so the parity oracle cannot be implemented as a partial-issue allowance.

section.section: 08a ŌĆö Clinical Prescription Dispense (NOT-GIVEN ŌåÆ GIVEN, stock decrement, FEFO lot-trace)
section.overview:
## What this slice builds

This slice closes the explicit stock-decrement TODO deferred since inc-05/inc-06 (`Prescription.java:534-537`; `PrescriptionPort.java:73`). It implements the NOT-GIVEN ŌåÆ GIVEN dispense transition in full: the four in-order guards from legacy, the `issuePharmacy` stamp, the hard negative-stock gate, aggregate `PharmacyMedicine.stock` decrement, the append-only `PharmacyStockCard` OUT row, in-memory FEFO batch consumption over `PharmacyMedicineBatch`, and `PrescriptionBatch` lot-trace rows.

### Prerequisite: new `clinical::api` published dispense port (architecture task, BEFORE 08a code)

`PrescriptionPort` is currently intra-module (`clinical.application`). The `pharmacy` module cannot legally call it. A new published port must be added to `clinical::api` (`@NamedInterface("api")`) following the `billing::api` / `SettlementDispatcher` pattern ŌĆö no reverse edge, no cycle. The port exposes:
- `readForDispense(String prescriptionUid) ŌåÆ PrescriptionDispenseView` ŌĆö read-only projection of the fields the pharmacy service needs.
- `completeDispense(String prescriptionUid, String issuePharmacyUid, TxAuditContext ctx)` ŌĆö calls the existing `Prescription.issue()` aggregate method and persists; must run in the caller's transaction (propagation REQUIRED, no REQUIRES_NEW).

Until this port is built and the `pharmacy ŌåÆ clinical::api` Modulith edge is declared, the `PharmacyDispenseService` cannot be wired. This is a **hard prerequisite**; 08a cannot freeze without it.

### Pharmacy worklist ŌĆö FILTER only (Q1)

The worklist is a FILTER: it returns NOT-GIVEN prescriptions whose linked `PatientBill` meets the patient-type settlement criterion:
- OUTPATIENT / OUTSIDER: `BillStatus` IN {`PAID`, `COVERED`} (`PatientResource.java:4347`, `PatientResource.java:4364`)
- INPATIENT: `BillStatus` IN {`PAID`, `COVERED`, `VERIFIED`} (`PatientResource.java:4381`, `PatientResource.java:4410`)

The dispense terminal itself has **no bill-status check** (`PatientResource.java:3199-3293` ŌĆö no `getPatientBill().getStatus()` call). The terminal is bypassable by direct call; that is the legacy behaviour. A hard dispense gate is an owner-elected CR (Q1 OWNER_RELEASE_GATE). `BillingQueries.getBillStatus` must NOT be called inside the dispense method unless and until that gate is owner-approved.

### Stock model re-modelling (data-architect prerequisite)

Legacy has a plain `double PharmacyMedicine.stock` scalar with NO `StockBalance` entity and NO database locking (`fefo-stock lane, PharmacyMedicine.java:38`). The baseline reproduces the scalar + single-ledger semantics using `BigDecimal` (pre-approved doubleŌåÆBigDecimal change). The pessimistic `PESSIMISTIC_WRITE` lock and `@Version` on `PharmacyMedicineBatch` are BLOCKED pending ADR-0017 ratification (Q4). The FEFO NULLS-LAST sort is BLOCKED pending HDE sign-off (Q8). Both are spec'd below with `frozen:false`.

### Guard execution order (legacy ordering preserved verbatim)

Legacy `PatientResource.java:3199-3293` applies guards in this order and this order only:
1. Prescription exists (NOT_FOUND if absent).
2. `status == NOT-GIVEN` ŌĆö else 422 `"not a pending prescription"`.
3. `issued` valid: `> 0` AND `<= balance` ŌĆö else 422 `"Invalid issue value"`.
4. `issued == qty` (all-or-nothing) ŌĆö else 422 `"You can only issue the prescribed qty"`.
5. Hard negative-stock gate: `PharmacyMedicine.stock < qty` ŌĆö else 422 `INSUFFICIENT_STOCK`.

Guards 1ŌĆō4 execute inside `Prescription.issue()` (as already built). Guard 5 belongs to the pharmacy service and runs AFTER the clinical domain method sets status to GIVEN (legacy ordering: `PatientResource.java:3237` status set; `PatientResource.java:3243` stock check). The modern impl may reorder guard 5 to run BEFORE the `completeDispense` call (a correctness improvement); if so that reordering must be explicitly logged as a deliberate deviation from the legacy sequencing. The `@Transactional` boundary still rolls back all mutations on any failure.

### Medicine pricing via `billing::api`

Medicine charges are routed through `BillingCommands.recordClinicalCharge(ChargeRequest{kind=MEDICINE, serviceUid=medicineUid, qty=prescribedQty, ...}, ctx)` at **prescribe time** (already implemented in `PrescriptionService`). The dispense step does NOT re-invoke billing; it only stamps `issuePharmacyUid` and decrements stock. `BillingCommands.java:34` ŌĆö propagation REQUIRED, no REQUIRES_NEW.

### FEFO algorithm ŌĆö verbatim legacy (null-expiry exclusion + id-ASC tiebreak)

Legacy `getEarlierBatch` (`PatientResource.java:3338-3376`):
- Fetch all `PharmacyMedicineBatch` rows with `qty > 0` for the (pharmacy, medicine) pair ŌĆö **unordered query**, sort in application memory.
- If ANY batch has a non-null `expiryDate`: select the batch with the **earliest** `expiryDate`; **silently exclude** all null-expiry batches.
- If NO batch has an `expiryDate`: fall back to the batch with the **lowest `id`** (FIFO insertion order).

The NULLS-LAST SQL ordering (`expiry_date ASC NULLS LAST`) is a **behaviour change** (would allow null-expiry lots to be consumed when dated stock exists) and is BLOCKED pending HDE sign-off (Q8). The id-ASC tiebreak is pinned by the decisions doc and IS frozen.

`deductBatch` recurses: for each consumed batch it decrements `PharmacyMedicineBatch.qty` in-place and writes a `PrescriptionBatch` lot-trace row. Legacy wraps the entire body in `try/catch(Exception e){//nothing}` ŌĆö batch shortfall is silent (`PatientResource.java:3296-3336`). The baseline may NOT silently swallow exceptions; a batch shortfall that causes `getEarlierBatch` to return `null` must surface as an `INSUFFICIENT_STOCK` error. This is a deliberate correction of a legacy defect (logged, not a parity assertion).

### New `ErrorCode` values required (shared kernel change)

- `INSUFFICIENT_STOCK` ŌĆö 422 Unprocessable Entity ŌĆö `"urn:hmis:error:insufficient-stock"` ŌĆö "Insufficient stock to fulfil this request".
- (No `STALE_ENTITY` needed for 08a baseline; it is gated on Q4/ADR-0017.)

### RBAC

Legacy `PatientResource.java:3199-3203`: **no `@PreAuthorize`** on `issue_medicine`. The rebuild adds deny-by-default closure (approved hardening, ADR-0006, labelled net-new). The legacy 35-code set adds zero new codes for inc-08 (Q5). Dispense endpoint requires an authenticated JWT; no privilege-code gate for dispense is in the legacy 35-code set. Do NOT cite "177".

### Flyway

All new tables (`pharmacy_medicines`, `pharmacy_medicine_batches`, `pharmacy_stock_cards`) start at **V39**. Sequences (`seq_ppr_no` etc.) and `md_document_types` rows are already seeded in V13/V14 ŌĆö do not re-create them.

check.section: 08a ŌĆö rx-dispense (Clinical Prescription Dispense: NOT-GIVEN ŌåÆ GIVEN, stock decrement, FEFO lot-trace)
check.reason:
The drafted rx-dispense section is charter-compliant: it smuggles NO deviation into the frozen baseline, every frozen:true criterion is backed by a real legacy cite that I corroborated against 00-discovery-raw.json and the as-built code, and every legitimately-blocked deviation is correctly marked frozen:false behind the right gate. I verified all three adversarial axes against ground truth.

ITEM 1 (no smuggled deviations) ŌĆö PASS on every named trap:
- Hard dispense gate: AC-RX-16 affirmatively FORBIDS calling BillingQueries.getBillStatus inside the dispense method and states the terminal has no bill-status check. This matches the as-built BillingQueries.java seam doc (D:\My_Works\HMS\HMSCLEAN2\backend\src\main\java\com\otapp\hmis\billing\api\BillingQueries.java lines 12-21) which restricts getBillStatus to the add_report bill-gate ONLY and mandates the local settled flag for settlement gates. Honours Q1.
- Pessimistic lock (Q4): AC-RX-12 is frozen:false, gated on CR-08-Q4 + ADR-0017 ratification; the overview repeats this; no STALE_ENTITY added to the baseline ErrorCode requirement. Correct.
- FEFO NULLS-LAST (Q8): AC-RX-13 is frozen:false (HDE gate); AC-RX-11 freezes the legacy null-expiry EXCLUSION + id-ASC tiebreak, which I confirmed verbatim at 00-discovery-raw.json (getEarlierBatch finding, PatientResource.java:3338-3376) and against ratified Q8. Correct.
- pharmacy_staff / affiliation: AC-RX-25 explicitly asserts NO affiliation check and NO pharmacy_staff lookup (Q2). AC-RX-15 makes pharmacyUid a required server-validated param used only to select the stock source (Q2). Correct.
- '177' codes / per-transition SoD / PRESCRIPTION-ALL gate: AC-RX-19 states no dispense privilege exists in the 35-code legacy set, requires only a valid JWT, labels deny-by-default as net-new hardening (ADR-0006), and the overview explicitly says 'Do NOT cite 177'. The term '177' appears nowhere in the draft. Confirmed against rbac-scoping lane (legacy issue_medicine has no @PreAuthorize; PRESCRIPTION-ALL is unverified/never-gated). Correct (Q5).
- FEFO-on-OTC / OTC PK-suffix / server-authoritative RO numbers: none appear in this clinical-Prescription section (OTC and RO numbering are out of scope for rx-dispense and parked as CR-08-FEFO-ON-OTC / CR-09-NUM1 / CR-Q10 in 08b/Q9). The cites array correctly records PatientResource.java:6317 (OTC deductBatch COMMENTED OUT) as confirming OTC has no FEFO. Correct.

ITEM 2 (frozen:true backed + authorized) ŌĆö PASS:
- AC-RX-04/05/06 guard messages, AC-RX-07 negative-stock 422, AC-RX-08 GIVEN/issued/balance/issuePharmacy/approved stamps, AC-RX-09 stock decrement, AC-RX-10 stock-card OUT row + reference string, AC-RX-11 FEFO, AC-RX-17/18 worklist filters, AC-RX-22 PharmacyMedicine-not-found, AC-RX-23 ledger completeness ŌĆö all trace to PatientResource.java cites I corroborated in the rx-lifecycle and fefo-stock lanes, and each is authorized by ratified Q1/Q2/Q5/Q8.
- AC-RX-14 (surface batch-shortfall as INSUFFICIENT_STOCK instead of legacy empty-catch swallow) is correctly frozen:true AND explicitly labelled a deliberate defect-correction (not a parity assertion), consistent with N7 and the discovery note at 00-discovery-raw.json. Acceptable because the legacy empty-catch is a confirmed defect, the correction is logged, and it does not change which lot or how much stock is consumed on the happy path.
- AC-RX-20 INSUFFICIENT_STOCK addition: I confirmed ErrorCode.java does NOT currently contain INSUFFICIENT_STOCK (only NO_DAY_OPEN..MISSING_INSURANCE_INFORMATION) and that InvalidPatientOperationException maps to BUSINESS_RULE (422), so AC-RX-04/05/06 asserting BUSINESS_RULE is correct.
- AC-RX-01/02 prerequisite: confirmed PrescriptionPort.java is intra-module (package clinical.application, javadoc 'Public intra-module boundary'), so the new clinical::api published dispense port is a genuine prerequisite. The BigDecimal money model is the pre-approved doubleŌåÆBigDecimal change.

ITEM 3 (BLOCKED items frozen:false) ŌĆö PASS: AC-RX-12 (Q4 lock) and AC-RX-13 (Q8 NULLS-LAST) are both frozen:false with the correct gate; CR-09-NUM1 (OTC suffix) is not in this section's scope, correctly.

Verdict UPHELD. The corrections below are ADVISORY (clarity/cite-accuracy/cross-cutting design) ŌĆö none is a charter or baseline-fidelity violation, so none blocks the freeze of this section. They should be addressed for precision and routed to the solution-architect where noted.
corrections: ADVISORY (cite/accuracy ŌĆö AC-RX-04): The criterion says detail text 'matching not a pending prescription (verbatim legacy message)'. The as-built Prescription.issue() actually throws 'Could not issue medicine. Prescription is not a pending prescription' (D:\My_Works\HMS\HMSCLEAN2\backend\src\main\java\com\otapp\hmis\clinical\domain\Prescription.java:514-516), and the legacy itself interpolates the prescription id ('... is not a pending prescription'). Neither legacy nor as-built emits the bare string, so 'verbatim' is inaccurate. Reword the AC to a CONTAINS/substring assertion on 'not a pending prescription' (and align AC-RX-05 'Invalid issue value' which IS emitted verbatim at Prescription.java:522/526, and AC-RX-06 'You can only issue the prescribed qty' verbatim at :530). This avoids a brittle equality test that the existing domain method would fail.

ADVISORY (cross-cutting design ŌĆö AC-RX-17/AC-RX-18, route to solution-architect): The worklist filters on three-valued live BillStatus resolved from patientBillUid (PAID/COVERED for OUT/OUTSIDER; +VERIFIED for INPATIENT). But the published BillingQueries seam doc (BillingQueries.java:12-21) states 'Settlement gates ... worklist filters MUST keep using the local settled flag ŌĆö this seam is for add_report bill-gating only', while RECONCILIATION ┬¦C (D18) confirms a binary settled flag CANNOT represent the COVERED/VERIFIED distinction the legacy worklist depends on. The spec does not state HOW the worklist obtains three-valued status without either (a) the insufficient binary settled flag or (b) a getBillStatus call the BillingQueries contract reserves for add_report. This is not a baseline-fidelity break (the legacy filter behaviour is reproduced correctly) but an unresolved mechanism question that must be settled with the solution-architect before AC-RX-17/18 can be implemented ŌĆö likely a new billing::api read seam (e.g. a worklist-status projection) rather than a settled-flag widening, since ADR-0008 ┬¦6 governs cross-module reads.

ADVISORY (cite precision ŌĆö AC-RX-04): The cite 'Prescription.java:514-517' should be 'Prescription.java:514-516' (the throw spans 514-516; 517 is the closing brace). Minor; tighten for traceability.

ADVISORY (scope-fidelity guard ŌĆö AC-RX-10): The reference-string criterion freezes 'Issued in prescription: id ' + <internal correlation id>. The legacy reference interpolates the legacy DB id (PatientResource.java:3252-3264, 'Issued in prescription: id <n>'). Per ADR-0014 ┬¦1 the raw internal id must never be exposed; the AC correctly says 'internal correlation id', but to prevent an implementer re-exposing the PK (the same trap CR-09-NUM1 guards for OTC numbers), add an explicit note that the correlation id in the reference string MUST NOT be the exposed PK/uid round-trip of any externally visible identifier and is for ledger trace only. Not a deviation as written ŌĆö just close the door.

ADVISORY (sequencing clarity ŌĆö guard-5 reorder, overview ┬¦'Guard execution order'): The overview permits reordering guard 5 (negative-stock) to run BEFORE completeDispense as a 'correctness improvement' that 'must be explicitly logged as a deliberate deviation from the legacy sequencing'. AC-RX-07 already asserts the correct OUTCOME (422 + no state change + status stays NOT-GIVEN), which is satisfied by the @Transactional rollback regardless of intra-tx ordering. Recommend stating that the legacy persisted-GIVEN-before-stock-check ordering (PatientResource.java:3237 before :3243) is an observable-equivalent under transactional rollback, so the reorder is NOT a behaviour change and need only be a code comment ŌĆö avoid labelling a behaviourally-invisible reorder as a 'deviation', which could imply a CR is needed when it is not.

section.section: Inc-08a ŌĆö Pharmacy stock model + schema (V39+): PharmacyMedicine aggregate balance, StockBatch (FEFO), StockMovement/stock-card ledger
section.overview:
## What this slice builds

The persistent stock substrate for the pharmacy bounded context, as a **modern re-model of the legacy single-ledger + scalar-stock structure** (ratified **D16-CORRECTED**). Three tables in a new `V39__pharmacy_stock_core.sql` migration (migrations resume at **V39**; the doc sequences `seq_*_no` are already seeded in V13 and are NOT touched here):

1. **`pharmacy_medicine` ŌĆö aggregate scalar balance.** A 1:1 re-model of legacy `PharmacyMedicine.stock` (a plain `double`, `PharmacyMedicine.java:38`), the **authoritative** read-modify-write aggregate keyed by `(pharmacy, medicine)`. Promoted to a typed `stock NUMERIC(19,6)` (doubleŌåÆBigDecimal is the pre-approved ADR-0009 ┬¦3 / engagement-memory data-type change). **No separate `StockBalance` entity** ŌĆö the scalar lives here exactly as legacy (D6/D16: the plan's `StockBalance` two-table split is rejected). Parity note: this column IS the legacy aggregate; the ledger and batches do NOT derive it.

2. **`stock_batch` ŌĆö per-lot FEFO inventory.** Re-model of legacy `PharmacyMedicineBatch` (`PharmacyMedicineBatch.java:35-53`): `(pharmacy, medicine)` key, free-text `batch_no`, nullable `manufactured_date`/`expiry_date`, qtyŌåÆ`NUMERIC(19,6)`. **CORRECTION flagged (not legacy fact):** legacy has a SINGLE in-place-decremented `qty`; this slice splits it into `received_qty` + `remaining_qty` (both `NUMERIC(19,6)`) as a derived modern split. The parity invariant is that **`remaining_qty` reproduces the legacy in-place `qty` after every decrement** (`deductBatch` sets `batch.qty = batch.qty - qty`, `PatientResource.java:3296-3336`); `received_qty` is the immutable lot intake. `received_qty`/`remaining_qty` are net-new derived columns, recorded as a data-type CR, NOT presented as 1:1 legacy fields.

3. **`stock_movement` ŌĆö append-only stock-card ledger.** Re-model of legacy `PharmacyStockCard` (`PharmacyStockCard.java:37-57`): `qty_in`/`qty_out`/`running_balance` (all `NUMERIC(19,6)`), free-text `reference`, `occurred_at`. **`running_balance` is a STORED post-movement snapshot of `pharmacy_medicine.stock`, NOT recomputed from prior rows** ŌĆö faithful to legacy (`balance` = snapshot after movement). **Net-new typed `movement_type` enum** mapped EXACTLY onto the legacy free-text reference strings so the stock-card report keeps parity (legacy has NO type column ŌĆö the type was conveyed only by the `reference` string). Append-only at the DB level.

### Data-architect sign-off framing & parity note (ratified D16-CORRECTED)

This is presented as an **acceptable modern re-model**, NOT as reproduced legacy structure. The single-ledger + scalar semantics are preserved and must be proven by a parity test:
- exactly ONE ledger table (`stock_movement`) Ōåö legacy's single `PharmacyStockCard` ŌĆö NO second balance ledger;
- the scalar `pharmacy_medicine.stock` is the sole authoritative aggregate Ōåö legacy `PharmacyMedicine.stock` ŌĆö NOT derived from batches or movements;
- `running_balance` snapshot semantics Ōåö legacy `balance` snapshot;
- the `movement_type` enum decodes 1:1 to the legacy reference strings (parity-critical for the stock-card report).

The net-new deltas (typed enum, `received_qty`/`remaining_qty` split, `@Version`/audit columns on batches, optional concurrency locking) are explicitly labelled net-new, NOT parity assertions.

### JPA design

All three entities extend the shared-kernel `AuditableEntity` (hidden `BIGINT id` + `CHAR(26) uid` + `@Version` + `created_*`/`updated_*`), matching every inc-02..06 table. Cross-module references use **loose `*_uid` columns with NO physical FK** (the established convention ŌĆö see `prescriptions.medicine_uid`, `issue_pharmacy_uid`): `pharmacy_uid`/`medicine_uid` point at masterdata, which is a different module. The `stock_batch ŌåÆ pharmacy_medicine` relationship is INTRA-module so it CAN be a real FK; I spec it as a real FK on the `(pharmacy_uid, medicine_uid)` natural key via a surrogate FK to `pharmacy_medicine.id`. `coefficient` columns elsewhere are `NUMERIC(19,6)` (matching the seeded `item_medicine_coefficients`); no coefficient column belongs in 08a stock core (coefficient applies only on the pharmacyŌåöstore path in 08b).

### What is NOT in this section (boundaries honoured)

- **No FEFO null-expiry NULLS-LAST behaviour** ŌĆö the FEFO query index supports `expiry_date ASC, id ASC`, but the **selection semantics reproduce the legacy null-expiry EXCLUSION** (Q8/N8). NULLS-LAST is BLOCKED pending ADR-0017 + HDE sign-off; the index column ordering is pinned but the query's null handling stays legacy-exclusion until then.
- **No pessimistic `SELECT FOR UPDATE` / lock columns beyond the inherited `@Version`** (Q4/CR-08-Q4, BLOCKED pending ADR-0017). The `@Version` from `AuditableEntity` is inherited (net-new vs legacy's no-`@Version` batch), but the decrement-time lock is NOT specced here.
- **No OTC `PSO/{n}`/`PCST{n}` numbering or sequences** ŌĆö out of this section's scope and gated by CR-09-NUM1; not added to V39.
- **No `pharmacy_staff` table** (Q2) and **no movement-type `WASTAGE`** value (no legacy disposal path exists).
- **No `salesPharmacy` split**, no destination-batch-on-p2p (08b / CR-Q7).

section.overview:
## What this slice builds

This spec covers the **walk-in OTC `PharmacySaleOrder` lifecycle** within the `pharmacy` bounded-context module (`com.otapp.hmis.pharmacy`), migrating from the legacy scattering across `PatientServiceImpl`, `PharmacyResource`, and `PatientBillResource` into a self-contained pharmacy domain aggregate.

### Legacy-accurate behaviour (supersedes the planning doc on every drift point)

**Entities.** `PharmacySaleOrder` (header) + `PharmacySaleOrderDetail` (lines) + `PharmacyCustomer` (walk-in record, completely separate from the clinical `Patient`). Each line is billed via its own `PatientBill` against a **GENERAL dummy patient** (no='GENERAL'), not against the walk-in `PharmacyCustomer`. The header has an eager FK to `PharmacyCustomer`, `Pharmacy`, and `Pharmacist`.

**Header state machine.** `PENDING ŌåÆ APPROVED ŌåÆ ARCHIVED` (+ `PENDING ŌåÆ CANCELED`). States map verbatim from `PatientServiceImpl.java:3019-3026,3341,3079,3138,3199,3217`.
- `PENDING` is the initial state on order creation.
- `PENDING ŌåÆ APPROVED` is a **side effect** of the cashier paying the linked `PatientBill` row(s) via `POST /bills/confirm_bills_payment` (`PatientBillResource.java:269-393`). There is no standalone approve endpoint. The pharmacy module receives this signal via a `BillSettledEvent`-pattern integration event emitted by billing when bills are paid.
- `PENDING ŌåÆ CANCELED` is allowed by an explicit cancel call (guard: status == PENDING). Auto-cancel sweeps any PENDING order older than 24 hours (`PatientServiceImpl.java:3207-3229`).
- `APPROVED ŌåÆ ARCHIVED` is allowed when all details are `GIVEN`. Auto-archive sweeps any APPROVED order older than 24 hours whose details are all GIVEN (`PatientServiceImpl.java:3176-3205`). Archive is also triggerable manually.
- `ARCHIVED` and `CANCELED` are terminal; no transitions out.

**Detail state fields.** Each `PharmacySaleOrderDetail` carries TWO independent status fields (`PatientServiceImpl.java:3395-3442`; `PharmacySaleOrderDetail.java:41-42`):
1. **Fulfilment status**: `NOT-GIVEN` (set on line-save, not PENDING) ŌåÆ `GIVEN` (set on successful dispense).
2. **payStatus**: `UNPAID` (set on line-save) ŌåÆ `PAID` (set as bulk side effect of `confirm_bills_payment`).

**Dispense gate.** `give_medicine` (`PatientResource.java:6230-6232`) hard-rejects if `order.status != APPROVED`. It **never re-checks `payStatus`** at dispense time. Payment enforcement is indirect: the order only reaches APPROVED via the billing payment handler. The dispense terminal checks only `header.status == APPROVED` and `detail.status == NOT-GIVEN`.

**OTC dispense logic** (`PatientResource.java:6212-6322`):
1. Hard-reject if `order.status != APPROVED`.
2. Per detail: hard-reject if `detail.status != NOT-GIVEN`.
3. Force `issued = qty` (all-or-nothing, no partial quantity).
4. Reject if `balance > issued` or `issued <= 0`, or `issued != qty` ("You can only issue the specified qty").
5. Set `detail.status = GIVEN`, stamp `issuePharmacy` (NOT `salesPharmacy` ŌĆö `salesPharmacy` is a dead field never populated in legacy, `PharmacySaleOrderDetail.java:76`), stamp `approved` audit triplet.
6. Hard negative-stock guard: reject if `PharmacyMedicine.stock < qty` ("insufficient stock").
7. Decrement `PharmacyMedicine.stock` by qty.
8. Write `PharmacyStockCard` OUT row: `qtyIn=0`, `qtyOut=issued`, `balance=newStock`, `reference='Issued in sale: id <detailId>'`.
9. **`deductBatch` is COMMENTED OUT** (`PatientResource.java:6317`): no `PharmacyMedicineBatch` quantities are decremented and no lot-trace rows are written. This is the verified legacy behaviour; applying FEFO to OTC is parked as **CR-08-FEFO-ON-OTC**.

**Pricing.** Each detail's `PatientBill.amount = Medicine.price * qty` (flat cash price from `medicines.price`, NUMERIC(19,2), HALF_UP per ADR-0009 ┬¦4). `paymentType` is hardcoded to the literal string `CASH` regardless of the incoming value (`PatientServiceImpl.java:3342`). No insurance plan pricing, no co-pay, no discount for OTC. All OTC bills therefore carry `paymentType=CASH`, meaning the ADR-0010 pharmacy-sales reporting slice for OTC is all-CASH unless CR-09-NUM2 is approved.

**GENERAL dummy patient.** `savePharmacySaleOrder` ensures a `Patient` with `no='GENERAL'` exists, creating it if absent (`PatientServiceImpl.java:3259-3355`). Each OTC line's `PatientBill` is hung off this dummy patient. The pharmacy module must reproduce this dependency; the `GENERAL` patient is a billing anchor, not a walk-in customer record.

**Numbering.** `PharmacySaleOrder.no = 'PSO/' + id` (save-then-renumber off IDENTITY autoincrement, `PatientServiceImpl.java:3352`). `PharmacyCustomer.no`: two divergent legacy formats ŌĆö `'PCST' + id` (via `savePharmacySaleOrder`, `PatientServiceImpl.java:3313`) vs `'PCST/' + Year + '/' + id` (via `createPharmacyCustomer`, `PatientServiceImpl.java:3249`). The format strings are verified verbatim. However, using the raw entity PK as the numeric suffix violates **ADR-0014 ┬¦1** (no exposed internal id). The fix ŌĆö backing the suffix with dedicated sequences `seq_pso_no` and `seq_pcst_no` (new, **V39+**) ŌĆö is **CR-09-NUM1** (pending owner approval). Until approved, the format strings can be declared but the AC for sequence-backing is frozen:false.

**RBAC.** Legacy `give_medicine` and `save_pharmacy_sale_order`/`save_pharmacy_sale_order_detail` carry **no `@PreAuthorize`** (`PatientResource.java:3199-3203`, `6212-6216`). The modern baseline applies deny-by-default closure (ADR-0006 hardening, net-new, not a parity assertion). The 35 seeded privilege codes are the allow-list; no new codes are added by inc-08a. The exact assignment of which of the 35 codes gates which OTC endpoint is the coarse-gating decision (Q5 confirms reproduce-coarse-verbatim; per-transition SoD is CR-08-SoD).

**ADR-0010 reporting consequence.** Because `paymentType` is hardcoded `CASH` for every OTC bill (`PatientServiceImpl.java:3342`), the ADR-0010 pharmacy-sales revenue breakdown by payment mode will show all OTC revenue under CASH. Any reporting that tries to break OTC revenue by actual tendered payment mode will be incorrect until CR-09-NUM2 (honour real payment mode) is approved.

**Prerequisite.** A new **`clinical::api` published prescription read + dispense port** must be added to `clinical/api/` before the pharmacy module can read prescriptions across the module boundary. `PrescriptionPort` is today intra-module (`clinical.application.PrescriptionPort`); the pharmacy module cannot legally depend on it. This is a hard prerequisite for the clinical-dispense stories in 08a but NOT for the OTC stories ŌĆö OTC is entirely pharmacy-owned. The `DocumentNumberService` promotion to `shared.documentnumber` is already done (Q11, as-built).

### As-built anchors (do NOT rebuild)
- `shared.documentnumber.DocumentNumberService` + `DocumentType` enum (`DocumentType.java:30`) ŌĆö already promoted to shared kernel. Add `PSO` and `PCST` constants with `seq_pso_no`/`seq_pcst_no` sequences when CR-09-NUM1 is approved.
- `billing::api.BillingCommands.recordClinicalCharge` (`BillingCommands.java:34`) ŌĆö route OTC line billing through this seam (kind=MEDICINE, serviceUid=medicineUid).
- `shared.error.ErrorCode` ŌĆö add `INSUFFICIENT_STOCK` (422) for the negative-stock gate; `STALE_ENTITY` (409) for optimistic-lock surface.
- `AuditableEntity` ŌĆö all new entities extend this (`@Version` optimistic lock already on superclass).
- Flyway baseline at **V38**; all OTC-related migrations start at **V39**.
- `seq_pso_no` and `seq_pcst_no` sequences do NOT yet exist; they require a V39+ migration (gated on CR-09-NUM1).

check.reason: The acceptance criteria are overwhelmingly charter-faithful: no smuggled frozen deviations were found. Every forbidden item is correctly absent or correctly parked ŌĆö the dispense gate is a STATUS-machine gate (header==APPROVED, never a bill-pay/getBillStatus check; AC-OTC-06 overview is explicit it never re-checks payStatus); FEFO-on-OTC is asserted ABSENT (AC-OTC-09); no NULLS-LAST or pessimistic lock is frozen anywhere; no pharmacy_staff/affiliation gate (AC-OTC-24 is pharmacyUid required+validated only, Q2-faithful); RO numbers are not server-authoritative for OTC; per-transition SoD is deferred to CR-08-SoD (AC-OTC-25); the 35-code allow-list is cited, never "177"; and the PK-suffix numbering ACs (AC-OTC-19/20/21) are correctly marked frozen:false and listed in blockedItems against CR-09-NUM1. However, TWO defects block UPHELD. (1) BLOCKING ŌĆö the "As-built anchors" instruction "route OTC line billing through BillingCommands.recordClinicalCharge (kind=MEDICINE, serviceUid=medicineUid)" SMUGGLES a deviation into the build path: recordClinicalCharge runs the two-step cash-first/insurance-override pricing engine plus the PENDING-invoice accumulator (verified BillingCommands.java:24-34, ChargeRequest.java:27-37 carries planUid/membershipNo). Routing OTC through it would subject OTC bills to plan-pricing/co-pay logic, directly contradicting Q9 and the spec's own frozen AC-OTC-02/AC-OTC-22 (flat Medicine.price*qty, paymentType=CASH literal, NO insurance plan lookup). The legacy OTC PatientBill is built inline with no pricing engine (PatientServiceImpl.java:3395-3442,3342). (2) ADVISORY/traceability ŌĆö AC-OTC-09 decisionRef=Q8 is mis-attributed: the no-FEFO-on-OTC behaviour is governed by Q9 / CR-08-FEFO-ON-OTC (Q8 governs the clinical/transfer FEFO null-expiry + NULLS-LAST mechanism, which does not touch the OTC path at all). The asserted behaviour and frozen:true are correct; only the decision reference is wrong. Fix both and the section is sound.
corrections: BLOCKING ŌĆö Overview 'As-built anchors' bullet: REMOVE the instruction to route OTC line billing through billing::api.BillingCommands.recordClinicalCharge. recordClinicalCharge executes the cash-first/insurance-override pricing engine and the PENDING-invoice accumulator (BillingCommands.java:24-34; ChargeRequest carries planUid/membershipNo), which would apply plan pricing/co-pay to OTC bills and violate Q9 + the section's own frozen AC-OTC-02/AC-OTC-22 (flat Medicine.price*qty, paymentType=CASH literal, no insurance lookup). The OTC PatientBill must be created with flat-price/CASH math and NOT pass through the plan-pricing seam. If a billing seam is needed for OTC, it must be a distinct flat-cash charge path (or escalate to solution-architect/legacy-analyst to define one) ŌĆö do not reuse recordClinicalCharge.

ADVISORY ŌĆö AC-OTC-09: change decisionRef from 'Q8' to 'Q9' (with explicit reference to CR-08-FEFO-ON-OTC). Q8 governs the clinical/transfer FEFO null-expiry exclusion + NULLS-LAST mechanism and does not apply to the OTC dispense path; the OTC no-batch-decrement behaviour is parked under CR-08-FEFO-ON-OTC per the Q9 decision. The behaviour assertion and frozen:true are correct and unchanged.

ADVISORY ŌĆö AC-OTC-26 decisionRef=Q5 is loose: the AuditRecorder/no-Envers audit mechanism is not a Q5 (RBAC) topic. Retag to the shared-kernel audit baseline / approved audit requirement rather than Q5. Behaviour (AuditRecorder, no @Audited) is correct.

ADVISORY ŌĆö AC-OTC-23 and AC-OTC-30 quote the discovery reconcile verdict 'OTC sales emit StockMovement rows' as supporting evidence; in the raw that verdict is ACCURATE_WITH_CORRECTIONS and the payment-mode/saleType enrichment is tagged NEW SCOPE (BILL-5 / CR-09-NUM2). The ACs already gate real-payment-mode behind CR-09-NUM2 and freeze only the all-CASH consequence, which is correct ŌĆö but soften the cite framing so it is not read as an unqualified confirmation of a frozen StockMovement schema.

section.section: inc-08a RBAC + Pharmacy Scoping (@PreAuthorize mapping + pharmacyUid resolution)
section.overview:
## What this slice specifies

The authorization layer for the inc-08a (pharmacy dispensing + stock core) endpoints: the `@PreAuthorize` privilege mapping reusing **only the 35 seeded privilege codes** (26 live + 9 dead, per `expected-privilege-codes.txt`), reproducing the **coarse legacy gating verbatim**, plus the `pharmacyUid` server-side resolution contract. Pharmacy/inventory are empty greenfield stubs, so every gate is authored here from the legacy oracle ŌĆö never invented.

## The legacy-accurate behaviour (the parity baseline)

Legacy authorization is **method-level `@PreAuthorize("hasAnyAuthority(...)")` only**, with **deliberately uneven coverage** (`SecurityConfig.java:79-95`, `CustomAuthorizationFilter.java:70-107`). For the 08a surface specifically:

- **Pharmacy catalog / stock-admin (only two LIVE pharmacy gates exist):** `POST /pharmacies/save` ŌåÆ `ADMIN-ACCESS` (`PharmacyResource.java:122`); `POST /pharmacies/update_stock` ŌåÆ `MEDICINE_STOCK-UPDATE` (`:200`). These are the ONLY active pharmacy authority gates in legacy.
- **Clinical dispense (`/patients/issue_medicine`) was UNGATED** ŌĆö no `@PreAuthorize` at all (`PatientResource.java:3199`; finding "No @PreAuthorize on dispense endpoints", `00-discovery-raw.json:98-99`). Any authenticated JWT bearer could call it; absent a Bearer token it was anonymously reachable (`SecurityConfig.java:89` commented + filter fall-through `CustomAuthorizationFilter.java:105-106`).
- **OTC walk-in sale flow was UNGATED** ŌĆö `save_pharmacy_sale_order` `@PreAuthorize` is COMMENTED OUT (`PharmacyResource.java:298`); `save_pharmacy_sale_order_detail`, `cancel`, `archive`, `archive_all`, list/get carry NO annotation (`PharmacyResource.java:297-345`).
- **Reads UNGATED:** `get_pharmacy_medicine_list` (`:143`, takes `@RequestParam pharmacy_name`), `get_pharmacy_medicine_batches` (`:165`, `@RequestParam pharmacy_id`), `get_prescription_batches`, `load_pharmacies_like` ŌĆö none carry `@PreAuthorize`.

**The "177" figure is the inc-06 phantom and is BANNED from acceptance criteria** (Q5; `01-RECONCILIATION-AND-SCOPE.md` D12). 177 counts `@PreAuthorize` *sites* (26 live, 9 commented-dead); the ratified IAM truth is **35 distinct codes**. inc-08 adds **zero** new codes.

## What the as-built HMSCLEAN2 already provides (do NOT rebuild, DO label)

- **Deny-by-default is ALREADY ACTIVE.** `SecurityConfig.java:126` carries `.anyRequest().authenticated()` (the catch-all the legacy *commented out*). Every 08a endpoint therefore inherits an authenticated() floor with **no extra code** ŌĆö but this is **ADR-0006 hardening, net-new, NOT a parity assertion**. It must never be framed as reproducing a legacy 403/401; legacy returned neither on the ungated flows.
- **Authorities map directly** from the `privileges` JWT claim with empty prefix (`SecurityConfig.java:85-92`), so `hasAnyAuthority('MEDICINE_STOCK-UPDATE')` resolves verbatim ŌĆö identical to legacy `CustomAuthorizationFilter.java:76-93`.
- **`PrivilegeGateArchTest`** already fails the build if any `@PreAuthorize` cites a code outside the 27 live codes (26 + BILL-A). 08a's gates must pass this test unchanged ŌĆö i.e. cite ONLY `ADMIN-ACCESS` / `MEDICINE_STOCK-UPDATE` for the in-scope live gates.

## The two RBAC decisions this spec freezes

1. **Coarse-verbatim gating (parity, Q5).** Reproduce the exact two live pharmacy gates; emit NO new privilege codes; per-transition SoD is **parked as CR-08-SoD** (do not author granular -VERIFY/-APPROVE codes for any 08a flow).
2. **Deny-by-default minimum on formerly-ungated flows (net-new, ADR-0006).** Legacy clinical dispense and the entire OTC flow were ungated/anonymous. The approved hardening floor is `authenticated()` (already enforced globally). The spec adds **no role gate** on top of those flows in the baseline ŌĆö to add one (e.g. gating dispense or OTC on a privilege) would be a parity deviation and is the **OPEN dispense-gate item tied to Q1** (owner-gated; if ever elected, via `SettlementPolicy` local-flag, NEVER `BillingQueries.getBillStatus`). `PRESCRIPTION-ALL` is a **never-gated catalogue string** ŌĆö it is NOT in the 35-code seed and MUST NOT be authored into any gate.

## pharmacyUid scoping (Q2 / ADR-0020)

`pharmacyUid` is the modern uid rename of the legacy client-supplied `pharmacy_name`/`pharmacy_id` param (`PharmacyResource.java:143,165,206`; `PatientServiceImpl.java:3334-3339`). It is a **REQUIRED, server-VALIDATED per-call param** used ONLY to select the stock source. The server rejects missing/unresolvable `pharmacyUid`, but performs **NO user-affiliation check** ŌĆö there is no `pharmacy_staff` table, no PharmacistŌåÆPharmacy field (`Pharmacist.java:44-72`, `Pharmacy.java:34-54`), no pharmacy JWT claim (`CustomAuthenticationFilter.java:76-88`), and no 403 on mismatch. Any affiliation gate is **CR-Q2** (parked, amends ADR-0020 ┬¦Decision.4).

spec.title: Inc-08a Build Spec ŌĆö Pharmacy Dispensing + Stock Core (EXACT-PROCESS baseline)
spec.scope: Freezes the clinical-prescription dispense path (NOT-GIVEN to GIVEN, stock decrement, stock-card ledger, FEFO lot-trace), the OTC PharmacySaleOrder lifecycle, the pharmacy stock model + schema (V39), and the RBAC + pharmacyUid scoping contract ŌĆö as a verbatim reproduction of verified legacy behaviour. Four items are explicitly BLOCKED and ship frozen:false behind named gates: stock locking (Q4/CR-08-Q4 + ADR-0017), FEFO NULLS-LAST lot-selection (Q8/HDE), the as-built inc-05 worklist contradiction (BA reconciliation), the OTC PK-suffix numbering provenance (Q9/CR-09-NUM1), and the stock-ledger immutability mechanism (security-architect). Prerequisite: new clinical::api published prescription read+dispense port; Q11 shared.documentnumber DocumentNumberService promotion already complete. Every modern improvement (hard dispense gate, pessimistic lock, NULLS-LAST, FEFO-on-OTC, pharmacy affiliation, real OTC payment mode) is held behind a named CR and NEVER written into the baseline.
spec.specBody:
# Inc-08a Build Spec ŌĆö Pharmacy Dispensing + Stock Core

**Charter:** "modern design, exact process". This is the **EXACT-PROCESS baseline**. Every frozen acceptance criterion reproduces verified legacy behaviour **verbatim**, anchored to a legacy `file:line` cite. The target stack (Java 21 / Spring Boot 3.3 / Spring Modulith / PostgreSQL 16) is adopted, and the only permitted deltas are (a) the pre-approved `double ŌåÆ BigDecimal` data-type change (NUMERIC(19,6) / NUMERIC(19,2)) and (b) explicitly-labelled net-new hardening. **No business rule, pricing formula, numbering format, state machine, or report output is invented, guessed, or silently improved.** Every tempting modern improvement is parked behind a named Change Request.

**Authority order:** `03-DECISIONS-RATIFIED.md` (ratified register) > `01-RECONCILIATION-AND-SCOPE.md` (ground-truth legacy model) > `00-discovery-raw.json` (lane extracts with cites). The original `08-pharmacy-inventory-procurement.md` planning doc is **DRIFTED** and is NOT a build authority ŌĆö build only what the ratified decisions confirm.

---

## 1. Scope ŌĆö what freezes now vs. what is BLOCKED

### 1.1 Freezes now (this document, 08a)
- **Prerequisite seam:** the new `clinical::api` published prescription **read + dispense port** (three published interfaces, package-private impls; new allowed Modulith edge `pharmacy ŌåÆ clinical::api`; no cycle).
- **Clinical prescription dispense:** NOT-GIVEN ŌåÆ GIVEN transition, four in-order legacy guards, `issuePharmacy` stamp, hard negative-stock gate, aggregate `PharmacyMedicine.stock` decrement, append-only `PharmacyStockCard`/`stock_movement` OUT row, in-memory FEFO batch consumption (legacy null-expiry **EXCLUSION** + id-ASC tiebreak), `PrescriptionBatch` lot-trace rows.
- **OTC `PharmacySaleOrder` lifecycle:** PENDING ŌåÆ APPROVED ŌåÆ ARCHIVED (+ PENDING ŌåÆ CANCELED), two independent detail status fields (fulfilment + payStatus), flat `Medicine.price ├Ś qty` cash pricing, `paymentType=CASH` literal, GENERAL dummy-patient billing anchor, 24h auto-cancel / auto-archive sweeps, OTC dispense with **no** batch decrement (deductBatch commented out in legacy).
- **Stock model + schema V39:** `pharmacy_medicine` (scalar aggregate balance), `stock_batch` (per-lot FEFO inventory), `stock_movement` (append-only stock-card ledger) ŌĆö a ratified modern re-model (**D16-CORRECTED**) of the legacy single-ledger + scalar-stock structure.
- **RBAC + pharmacyUid scoping:** coarse-verbatim `@PreAuthorize` mapping reusing **only the 35 seeded privilege codes** (inc-08 adds zero), deny-by-default `authenticated()` floor (net-new ADR-0006 hardening, labelled), and the required/server-validated `pharmacyUid` stock-source selector with **no** affiliation check.

### 1.2 BLOCKED (frozen:false) ŌĆö by which gate
| BLOCKED item | Gate | Baseline behaviour until gate clears |
|---|---|---|
| Stock decrement **pessimistic lock** + `@Version`/409 STALE_ENTITY split (Q4) | **CR-08-Q4 + ADR-0017 ratification** | Only inherited `@Version` from `AuditableEntity`; NO `SELECT FOR UPDATE`, NO lock column, NO STALE_ENTITY in the 08a ErrorCode set. |
| FEFO **NULLS-LAST** lot-selection (Q8) | **HDE sign-off + ADR-0017 ┬¦2** | Reproduce legacy null-expiry **EXCLUSION**; id-ASC tiebreak pinned. The "which lot dispenses" parity oracle cannot freeze. |
| As-built inc-05 worklist **show-all contradiction** | **BA + engagement-lead reconciliation** | Published `PrescriptionWorklistPort` is specified to the **legacy FILTER**; the inc-05 `pharmacyWorklist()`/repository finder/javadoc must be reconciled (correct the comment) OR show-all raised as an explicit CR before freeze. |
| OTC number **PKŌåÆsequence suffix** provenance (Q9) | **CR-09-NUM1 (ADR-0014-forced)** | Format strings `PSO/{n}`, `PCST{n}`, `PCST/{year}/{n}` are declared verbatim; the sequence-backed (`seq_pso_no`/`seq_pcst_no`) variant cannot freeze and the raw-PK suffix is forbidden by ADR-0014 ┬¦1. |
| `stock_movement` **immutability / role-grant** enforcement mechanism | **security-architect PHI/audit classification** | DDL is append-only by application contract (no UPDATE/DELETE repository surface); the precise immutability/grant mechanism is implemented to security-architect spec, not self-authored here. |

**Q1 dispense-gate** is `OWNER_RELEASE_GATE` but **filter-only is the clean default** and does NOT hard-block this freeze: acceptance criteria state filter-only, the parity harness must NOT assert a terminal rejection, and `BillingQueries.getBillStatus` is **forbidden** at the dispense terminal. If a hard gate is ever elected it is **only** via `SettlementPolicy.requireSettled()` on the local `settled` flag.

### 1.3 Root freeze gate
Per `03-DECISIONS-RATIFIED.md:32`, **`readyToFreezeBuildSpec = NO`** until: (1) **ADR-0017 ratified** (root gate ŌĆö unblocks Q4 lock + Q8 mechanism; engagement-lead reconciles ADR-0006/0008/0009/0014/0020 status headers); (2) **Q8 HDE NULLS-LAST** call lands; (3) **CR-09-NUM1** approved (Q9). Q1 freezes as filter-only by default (does not hard-block). The freezable slices in ┬¦3ŌĆō┬¦5 (everything not depending on those four gates) are **ready to freeze on their own merits** the moment the prerequisite seam (┬¦2) is built and the BA worklist reconciliation closes.

---

## 2. Prerequisite ŌĆö clinical::api published prescription read + dispense port

### 2.1 Q11 prerequisite (already complete)
`shared.documentnumber.DocumentNumberService` + `DocumentType` are **already promoted** to the shared kernel (`@ApplicationModule(OPEN)`, generic `nextval(:seqName)` keyed off an enum allow-list). No `pharmacy ŌåÆ billing` edge for numbering. **08a core issues no new document types** ŌĆö `PSO`/`PCST` constants and their sequences are added only when CR-09-NUM1 is approved (see ┬¦4).

### 2.2 The published seam (hard prerequisite, architecture task BEFORE 08a code)
`PrescriptionPort` is today intra-module in `clinical.application` (controller-only consumer ŌĆö `PrescriptionPort.java:11-21`); the `pharmacy` module cannot legally depend on it. Publish **three** uid-keyed interfaces in `com.otapp.hmis.clinical.api` (which already carries `@NamedInterface("api")` ŌĆö `clinical/api/package-info.java:11`), all returning published DTO records (no entity, no internal `id` ŌĆö ADR-0014 ┬¦1), implementations package-private in `clinical.application` (mirroring `ConsultationLookupImpl` ŌĆö `ConsultationLookup.java:16`):

1. **`PrescriptionReadPort`** ŌĆö `PrescriptionView getByUid(String)`; `List<PrescriptionBatchView> listBatches(String)`.
2. **`PrescriptionWorklistPort`** ŌĆö `List<PrescriptionView> dispenseWorklist(WorklistFilter)`. **THE FILTER**, applied server-side in clinical.
3. **`PrescriptionDispensePort`** ŌĆö `PrescriptionView markDispensed(String, DispenseConfirmation, TxAuditContext)`. The decrement seam: pharmacy performs its OWN stock decrement / FEFO / stock-card write, then calls back to flip NOT-GIVEN ŌåÆ GIVEN and stamp `approved_*` + `issue_pharmacy_uid`. Propagation **REQUIRED** (caller's tx), NO `@Async`, NO `REQUIRES_NEW` ŌĆö atomic with the pharmacy decrement (mirrors `BillingCommands.recordClinicalCharge` ŌĆö `BillingCommands.java:8-21`).

**Direction / no cycle.** The command edge inverts relative to billing: pharmacy ORCHESTRATES the dispense (it owns the `pharmacyUid`-selected stock source, Q2) and calls **down into `clinical::api`**. Add the single allowed edge `pharmacy ŌåÆ clinical::api`. There is **no `clinical ŌåÆ pharmacy` edge**, no reverse callback, no event from clinical to pharmacy. `ApplicationModules.verify()` stays green.

**Published DTOs (no entity leak):** `PrescriptionView` (mirror of `PrescriptionDto.java:18-81` minus `id`), `PrescriptionBatchView` (mirror of `PrescriptionBatchDto.java:14-25`), `WorklistFilter` (published `patientType` enum OUTPATIENT/OUTSIDER/INPATIENT ŌĆö NOT the leaked domain status), `DispenseConfirmation` (mirror of `IssueRequest.java:20-35` with `issuePharmacyUid` **required** ŌĆö pharmacy has already resolved/validated it, Q2).

### 2.3 THE reconciliation flag (must go to BA ŌĆö load-bearing) ŌĆö BLOCKED
The as-built inc-05 code **deliberately drops** the settled/bill-status filter for the pharmacy worklist (`PrescriptionPort.java:124-131` javadoc; `PrescriptionRepository.java:132-143` "settled filter intentionally omitted"; `PrescriptionService.java:362-366` returns all NOT-GIVEN with no bill-status predicate). This **contradicts** ratified **Q1** and the verified legacy non-empty FILTER (`PatientResource.java:4347/4364/4381/4410`). It is an un-CR'd silent behaviour change sitting in inc-05 code; **inc-08a must NOT freeze on top of it.** The new `PrescriptionWorklistPort` is specified to the legacy filter and the parity oracle asserts the filter, not show-all ŌĆö but the worklist parity oracle (AC-RX-PRE-05) cannot freeze until BA rules: (a) correct the inc-05 comment/finder to the legacy filter (most likely), or (b) confirm an approved show-all CR. This is the inc-06 "hard gate vs filter" lesson (RECONCILIATION N13) applied again.

### 2.4 What this seam explicitly does NOT do
No hard dispense gate / `BillingQueries.getBillStatus` at `markDispensed` (Q1); no `pharmacyUid` affiliation check / `pharmacy_staff` table (Q2); no FEFO/NULLS-LAST/lock semantics (those are pharmacy-module concerns, BLOCKED Q4/Q8); no new prescription states (only NOT-GIVEN/GIVEN ŌĆö `PrescriptionStatus.java:23-26`; D1 8-state phantom rejected; D3 salesPharmacy phantom rejected).

## 3. Clinical Prescription Dispense (NOT-GIVEN ŌåÆ GIVEN, stock decrement, FEFO lot-trace)

### 3.1 Overview
Closes the stock-decrement TODO deferred since inc-05/06 (`Prescription.java:534-537`; `PrescriptionPort.java:73`). The pharmacy-orchestrated dispense runs guards 1-4 inside `Prescription.issue()` (already built), then guard 5 (hard negative-stock gate) in the pharmacy service, then the aggregate decrement, the append-only stock-card OUT row, in-memory FEFO batch consumption, and `PrescriptionBatch` lot-trace rows ŌĆö finally calling `PrescriptionDispensePort.markDispensed` to flip clinical state.

**Guard execution order (legacy `PatientResource.java:3199-3293`, preserved):** (1) prescription exists else NOT_FOUND; (2) status==NOT-GIVEN else 422 "...not a pending prescription"; (3) issued > 0 AND **issued >= balance** else 422 "Invalid issue value"; (4) issued==qty (all-or-nothing) else 422 "You can only issue the prescribed qty"; (5) hard negative-stock gate `PharmacyMedicine.stock < qty` else 422 INSUFFICIENT_STOCK. Legacy persisted GIVEN before the stock check (`:3237` before `:3243`); under the `@Transactional` rollback boundary, reordering guard 5 to run before the clinical flip is **observable-equivalent** (rollback undoes both regardless of intra-tx ordering) and is therefore NOT a behaviour change ŌĆö implement as a code comment, not a logged CR.

**FEFO (verbatim legacy, `getEarlierBatch` `PatientResource.java:3338-3376`):** fetch all `stock_batch` rows with `remaining_qty > 0` for the (pharmacy, medicine) pair ŌĆö **unordered query, sort in memory**; if ANY batch has a non-null `expiry_date`, select earliest `expiry_date` and **silently EXCLUDE all null-expiry batches**; if NO batch has an expiry, fall back to lowest `id` (FIFO). NULLS-LAST is BLOCKED (Q8). The legacy `try/catch(Exception){}` empty-catch that silently swallowed batch shortfall (`:3296-3336`) is a confirmed defect; the baseline surfaces a batch shortfall (null from `getEarlierBatch`) as INSUFFICIENT_STOCK ŌĆö **labelled deliberate defect-correction, NOT a parity assertion** (does not change which lot or how much is consumed on the happy path).

**Pricing:** medicine charges route through `BillingCommands.recordClinicalCharge(kind=MEDICINE, serviceUid=medicineUid, ...)` at **prescribe time** (already implemented in `PrescriptionService`). The dispense step does NOT re-invoke billing.

**Worklist three-valued status (route to solution-architect):** AC-RX-DSP-17/18 filter on three-valued live `BillStatus` (PAID/COVERED; +VERIFIED for INPATIENT). The published `BillingQueries.getBillStatus` seam is reserved for `add_report` bill-gating only, and RECONCILIATION ┬¦C/D18 confirms a binary `settled` flag cannot represent the COVERED/VERIFIED distinction. The mechanism by which the worklist obtains three-valued status (likely a new `billing::api` worklist-status read projection, governed by ADR-0008 ┬¦6) must be settled with the solution-architect before AC-RX-DSP-17/18 are implemented. This is NOT a baseline-fidelity break (the legacy filter behaviour is correctly specified) ŌĆö it is an unresolved cross-module read mechanism.

## 4. OTC PharmacySaleOrder Lifecycle

### 4.1 Overview
Walk-in OTC `PharmacySaleOrder` lifecycle, migrated into the pharmacy module. Entities: `PharmacySaleOrder` (header) + `PharmacySaleOrderDetail` (lines) + `PharmacyCustomer` (walk-in record, **separate from clinical `Patient`**). Each line is billed via its own `PatientBill` against a **GENERAL dummy patient** (no='GENERAL'), NOT against the walk-in customer.

**Header state machine** (`PatientServiceImpl.java:3019-3026,3079,3138,3199,3217`): PENDING (initial) ŌåÆ APPROVED (a **side effect** of the cashier paying the linked `PatientBill` via `confirm_bills_payment` ŌĆö `PatientBillResource.java:269-393`; no standalone approve endpoint; received via a cross-module settlement event) ŌåÆ ARCHIVED (when all details GIVEN; also auto-archive after 24h). PENDING ŌåÆ CANCELED (explicit cancel, guard status==PENDING; auto-cancel after 24h). ARCHIVED/CANCELED terminal.

**Detail two independent status fields** (`PatientServiceImpl.java:3395-3442`; `PharmacySaleOrderDetail.java:41-42`): fulfilment status NOT-GIVEN ŌåÆ GIVEN; payStatus UNPAID ŌåÆ PAID (bulk side effect of `confirm_bills_payment`).

**Dispense gate** (`give_medicine` `PatientResource.java:6230-6232`): hard-rejects if `order.status != APPROVED`; **never** re-checks payStatus at dispense. Payment enforcement is indirect (order only reaches APPROVED via the billing payment handler). OTC dispense (`PatientResource.java:6212-6322`): force issued=qty (all-or-nothing); set detail GIVEN; stamp `issuePharmacy` (NOT `salesPharmacy` ŌĆö dead field, `PharmacySaleOrderDetail.java:76`); hard negative-stock gate (`:6272-6273`); decrement `PharmacyMedicine.stock`; write stock-card OUT row reference="Issued in sale: id <detailId>". **`deductBatch` is COMMENTED OUT (`:6317`) ŌĆö NO batch decrement, NO lot-trace on OTC.**

**Pricing (Q9):** each detail's `PatientBill.amount = Medicine.price ├Ś qty` (flat cash, NUMERIC(19,2), HALF_UP per ADR-0009 ┬¦4); `paymentType` hardcoded literal `CASH` regardless of input (`PatientServiceImpl.java:3342`); no insurance plan / co-pay / discount. **OTC bills MUST be created with this flat-price/CASH math directly and MUST NOT pass through `BillingCommands.recordClinicalCharge`** ŌĆö that seam runs the cash-first/insurance-override pricing engine + PENDING-invoice accumulator (`BillingCommands.java:24-34`; `ChargeRequest` carries planUid/membershipNo) and would apply plan pricing/co-pay to OTC, violating Q9 and AC-OTC-02/AC-OTC-22. If a billing seam is needed for OTC it must be a **distinct flat-cash charge path** (escalate to solution-architect / legacy-analyst to define one) ŌĆö do NOT reuse `recordClinicalCharge`.

**GENERAL dummy patient** (`PatientServiceImpl.java:3259-3355`): ensured-or-created; the OTC `PatientBill` hangs off this dummy patient as a billing anchor.

**Numbering (Q9 / CR-09-NUM1 BLOCKED):** format strings verbatim ŌĆö `PSO/{id}`, `PCST{id}` (savePharmacySaleOrder), `PCST/{year}/{id}` (createPharmacyCustomer) ŌĆö but the raw-PK suffix violates ADR-0014 ┬¦1; the fix (back the suffix with `seq_pso_no`/`seq_pcst_no`, V39+) is **CR-09-NUM1** (owner approval pending). Until approved the format strings are declared but the sequence-backing AC is frozen:false.

**ADR-0010 reporting consequence:** because `paymentType` is hardcoded CASH, OTC revenue-by-payment-mode reports show all OTC under CASH until **CR-09-NUM2** (honour real payment mode) is approved.

## 5. Pharmacy Stock Model + Schema (V39)

### 5.1 Overview ŌĆö ratified modern re-model (D16-CORRECTED)
A modern re-model of the legacy single-ledger + scalar-stock structure, presented as an **acceptable re-model NOT reproduced structure**, proven by a parity test. Three tables in `V39__pharmacy_stock_core.sql` (migrations resume at **V39**; the `seq_*_no` doc sequences are already in V13 and are NOT touched):

1. **`pharmacy_medicine`** ŌĆö the **authoritative scalar aggregate** balance, 1:1 re-model of legacy `PharmacyMedicine.stock` (`PharmacyMedicine.java:38`), promoted to `stock NUMERIC(19,6)` (pre-approved doubleŌåÆBigDecimal, ADR-0009 ┬¦3). **No separate `StockBalance` entity** (D6/D16 ŌĆö the plan's two-table split is rejected). This column IS the legacy aggregate; the ledger and batches do NOT derive it.
2. **`stock_batch`** ŌĆö per-lot FEFO inventory, re-model of `PharmacyMedicineBatch` (`PharmacyMedicineBatch.java:35-53`): `(pharmacy, medicine)` key, free-text `batch_no`, nullable `manufactured_date`/`expiry_date`. **CORRECTION flagged (net-new derived split, NOT a 1:1 legacy field):** legacy has a single in-place-decremented `qty`; this slice splits it into `received_qty` (immutable intake) + `remaining_qty` (the parity invariant: `remaining_qty` reproduces the legacy post-decrement `qty`).
3. **`stock_movement`** ŌĆö append-only stock-card ledger, re-model of `PharmacyStockCard` (`PharmacyStockCard.java:37-57`): `qty_in`/`qty_out`/`running_balance`, free-text `reference`, `occurred_at`. **`running_balance` is a STORED post-movement snapshot of `pharmacy_medicine.stock`, NOT recomputed.** A **net-new typed `movement_type` enum** maps 1:1 onto the legacy free-text reference strings so the stock-card report keeps parity (legacy has NO type column ŌĆö type was conveyed only by the reference string, which is still persisted verbatim).

**JPA:** all three entities extend `AuditableEntity` (hidden BIGINT id + CHAR(26) uid + `@Version` + audit cols). Cross-module references use loose `*_uid` columns with NO physical FK (`pharmacy_uid`/`medicine_uid` ŌåÆ masterdata, a different module). `stock_batch ŌåÆ pharmacy_medicine` is intra-module so it IS a real surrogate FK on `pharmacy_medicine.id` (no cascade ŌĆö legacy has none). No `coefficient` column in 08a stock-core (08b pharmacyŌåöstore path only).

**Net-new deltas (explicitly labelled, NOT parity assertions):** the typed `movement_type` enum value-set; the `received_qty`/`remaining_qty` split; the inherited `@Version`; the `ck_pharmacy_medicine_stock_nonneg` DB CHECK (legacy is a plain unconstrained double ŌĆö the genuinely-frozen parity element is the **app-layer 422 INSUFFICIENT_STOCK reject**, with the CHECK as net-new DB backstop).

## 6. RBAC + Pharmacy Scoping (@PreAuthorize mapping + pharmacyUid resolution)

### 6.1 Overview
Authorization for the 08a surface: `@PreAuthorize` mapping reusing **only the 35 seeded privilege codes** (26 live + 9 dead per `expected-privilege-codes.txt`), reproducing coarse legacy gating verbatim, plus the `pharmacyUid` resolution contract. Legacy authorization is method-level `@PreAuthorize("hasAnyAuthority(...)")` only, with deliberately uneven coverage.

**For the 08a surface specifically ŌĆö only two LIVE pharmacy gates exist:** `pharmacies/save ŌåÆ ADMIN-ACCESS` (`PharmacyResource.java:122`); `update_stock ŌåÆ MEDICINE_STOCK-UPDATE` (`:200`). **Scope fence:** the transfer/procurement codes (PHARMACY_ORDER-ALL, STORE_ORDER-ALL, GOODS_RECEIVED_NOTE-*, LOCAL_PURCHASE_ORDER-*) are live for **08b** and are already in `PrivilegeGateArchTest` LIVE_CODES ŌĆö "only two pharmacy gates" is an 08a statement, not a global inc-08 cap.

Clinical dispense (`issue_medicine` `PatientResource.java:3199`) and the entire OTC flow (`save_pharmacy_sale_order` `@PreAuthorize` COMMENTED OUT `PharmacyResource.java:298`; detail/cancel/archive/list/get unannotated) and reads were **UNGATED**. The "177" figure is the inc-06 phantom and is **BANNED** (Q5; D12) ŌĆö truth = 35 codes; inc-08 adds zero.

**As-built (do NOT rebuild):** deny-by-default is ALREADY ACTIVE (`SecurityConfig.java:126` `.anyRequest().authenticated()` ŌĆö the catch-all legacy commented out) = **ADR-0006 net-new hardening, NOT a parity assertion** (legacy returned anonymous success). Authorities map from the `privileges` JWT claim with empty prefix (`SecurityConfig.java:85-92`). `PrivilegeGateArchTest` fails the build on any code outside the 27 live codes. `PRESCRIPTION-ALL` is a **never-gated catalogue string** ŌĆö NOT in the seed, MUST NOT be authored.

**pharmacyUid (Q2 / ADR-0020):** the modern uid rename of the legacy client-supplied `pharmacy_name`/`pharmacy_id` param ŌĆö **REQUIRED, server-VALIDATED per-call**, used ONLY to select the stock source. Server rejects missing/unresolvable, but performs **NO affiliation check** ŌĆö no `pharmacy_staff` table, no PharmacistŌåÆPharmacy field, no pharmacy JWT claim, no 403 on mismatch. Affiliation gate = **CR-Q2** (parked).
